title: oreocnide frutescens subsp. nivea
intro: Oreocnide frutescens subsp. nivea, also known as white snake vine, is a species of flowering plant in the family Moraceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The herbal preparations of white snake vine typically involve drying and processing the leaves to be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although the plant's use is not widely recognized or recommended by modern medical professionals.
